projectDescription: An R-based machine learning project that predicts sumo match outcomes using Bayesian GLM and neural network models trained on wrestler attributes such as height, weight, age, and win history.
keyFeatures: Bayesian GLM (Generalized Linear Model) for match outcome prediction with ~70% accuracy
Neural network predictor using the neuralnet R package with configurable hidden layers
CSV-based wrestler data ingestion covering weight, wins, age, and height for both competitors
Train/test split evaluation with confusion matrix reporting via caret
R Markdown report generation (sumo.Rmd) for reproducible analysis and PDF output
Side-by-side model comparison: GLM vs neural network predictions on unseen matches
publicApiSurface: sumo.read(csv) — reads and preprocesses sumo match CSV data into a tibble
data.split(data, ratio) — splits a dataset into train/test subsets by ratio
normalize(x) — min-max normalizes a numeric vector
glm(result ~ ., history, family = 'binomial') — fits the binomial GLM on historical match data
neuralnet(result ~ ., history, hidden = 8) — trains a neural network with configurable hidden layers
predict.glm(bin, undecided) — generates GLM predictions for undecided matches
predict(nn, undecided) — generates neural network predictions for undecided matches
confusionMatrix(predicted, truth) — evaluates model accuracy via caret
